title: Filter out pre-existing files on startup

contextToReadFirst.reason: Current implementation — need to add baseline snapshot before stream creation

changes: In `startMonitoring()`, before creating the FSEvents stream, snapshot the current contents: `let existingFiles = Set(try FileManager.default.contentsOfDirectory(at: directory, ...).filter { $0.pathExtension == "m4a" })`
In the FSEvents callback, before handing off to the sync checker, check `if baselineFiles.contains(fileURL) { return }` — skip files that existed at startup
Edge case: if a file is deleted and re-added after startup, it should be detected. Remove files from `baselineFiles` if a deletion event is observed.

tests.name: test_ignoresPreExistingFiles
tests.description: Place two .m4a files in the temp directory before starting the watcher. Start the watcher. Assert onFileDetected does not fire for either. Then add a third .m4a file. Assert onFileDetected fires for only the third file.

tests.name: test_detectsReaddedFile
tests.description: Place a .m4a file, start the watcher, delete the file, then add it back. Assert onFileDetected fires for the re-added file.

doneWhen: Starting the watcher in a directory with existing .m4a files does not emit those files. Only new arrivals trigger events. Tests pass.
doNot: Remove the baseline filtering on restart — every fresh start of the watcher should re-snapshot. Persistence of 'seen files' is the state tracker's job (Story 3), not the watcher's.
